# Stellar Index ‚Äî Configuration Reference

This file is **generated** from the struct tags in
`internal/config/config.go` and regenerated by the
`docs-config` subcommand of `stellarindex-ops`.

To add / rename / remove a config option:

1. Edit `internal/config/config.go` (struct field + tags).
2. `make docs-config` (regenerates this file).
3. Commit both in the same PR.

Operators hand-edit a TOML file that maps onto this schema.
Secrets never live in the TOML file itself ‚Äî reference them via
the `env:` column.

## Top-level sections


### `[region]`

| Key | Type | Default | Env override | Description |
| --- | ---- | ------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| `region.id` | `string` | `r1` | ‚Äî | Short region identifier, lowercase (r1/r2/r3). |
| `region.name` | `string` | `London` | ‚Äî | Human-readable region name (London, Ashburn, ‚Ä¶). |
| `region.home_domain` | `string` | `stellarindex.io` | ‚Äî | DNS home domain for this org (used in stellar.toml + SCP quorum sub-quorum). |

### `[stellar]`

| Key | Type | Default | Env override | Description |
| --- | ---- | ------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| `stellar.network` | `string` | `pubnet` | ‚Äî | Network passphrase name ‚Äî pubnet / testnet / futurenet. |
| `stellar.core_http_endpoint` | `string` | `http://127.0.0.1:11626` | ‚Äî | stellar-core admin HTTP (used for liveness). |
| `stellar.rpc_endpoints` | `[]string` | `["http://127.0.0.1:8000"]` | ‚Äî | stellar-rpc endpoints for getEvents/getLedgers. Tried in order on failover. |
| `stellar.history_archive_url` | `string` | `https://history.stellar.org/prd/core-live/core_live_001` | ‚Äî | Public history archive (SDF or ours) for backfill catchup. |

### `[storage]`

| Key | Type | Default | Env override | Description |
| --- | ---- | ------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| `storage.postgres_dsn` | `string` | `postgres://stellarindex@127.0.0.1:5432/stellarindex?sslmode=disable` | `STELLARINDEX_POSTGRES_DSN` | Postgres DSN; password resolved via env: prefix. |
| `storage.redis_addr` | `string` | `127.0.0.1:6379` | ‚Äî | Redis master address host:port. Used when redis_sentinel_addrs is empty (single-node / direct mode). When sentinel addrs are set, this is ignored. |
| `storage.redis_sentinel_addrs` | `[]string` | `[]` | ‚Äî | List of Sentinel host:port addresses. Non-empty enables FailoverClient mode (production HA per ADR-0024); empty falls back to single-node redis_addr. |
| `storage.redis_master_name` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Sentinel master name as set in inventory (e.g. stellarindex-r1-cache). Required when redis_sentinel_addrs is non-empty. |
| `storage.redis_password_env` | `string` | _(required)_ | `STELLARINDEX_REDIS_PASSWORD` | Env var holding the Redis password (reference, not the password itself). Used as both requirepass (client auth) and SentinelPassword (sentinel auth) ‚Äî they're the same secret per the role. |
| `storage.redis_username` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Optional Redis ACL username. Empty (default) AUTHs as Redis's legacy 'default' user ‚Äî same wire shape as redis_password alone. Set to 'stellarindex' (or the operator's per-component user) when redis_acl_lockdown is enabled in the ansible role (F-1213 audit-2026-05-12); without a username the broker-side ACL rejects the connection. |
| `storage.s3_endpoint` | `string` | `http://127.0.0.1:9000` | ‚Äî | S3-compatible object-store endpoint (MinIO / AWS S3). |
| `storage.s3_region` | `string` | `r1` | ‚Äî | S3 region label (free-form for MinIO; AWS region name otherwise). |
| `storage.s3_bucket_archive` | `string` | `galexie-archive` | ‚Äî | Immutable history-archive bucket name. |
| `storage.s3_bucket_live` | `string` | `galexie-live` | ‚Äî | Live Galexie export bucket name. |
| `storage.s3_access_key_env` | `string` | `STELLARINDEX_S3_ACCESS_KEY` | ‚Äî | NAME of the env var holding the S3 access key ID (the value lives in that env var, not here). |
| `storage.s3_secret_key_env` | `string` | `STELLARINDEX_S3_SECRET_KEY` | ‚Äî | NAME of the env var holding the S3 secret access key (the value lives in that env var, not here). |
| `storage.s3_cold_endpoint` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Cold-tier S3 endpoint. Empty disables tiering. Production: https://s3.amazonaws.com |
| `storage.s3_cold_region` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Cold-tier S3 region. Production (aws-public-blockchain): us-east-1 |
| `storage.s3_cold_bucket_archive` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Cold-tier bucket + prefix for historical LCMs. Empty disables tiering. Production: aws-public-blockchain/v1.1/stellar/ledgers/pubnet |
| `storage.s3_cold_access_key_env` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Env var holding cold-tier S3 access key. Empty = anonymous reads (correct for public buckets). |
| `storage.s3_cold_secret_key_env` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Env var holding cold-tier S3 secret key. Empty = anonymous reads. |
| `storage.clickhouse_addr` | `string` | `127.0.0.1:9300` | ‚Äî | ClickHouse native address host:port for the Tier-1 lake (ADR-0034); used by the indexer real-time dual-sink. |
| `storage.clickhouse_live_sink` | `bool` | `true` | ‚Äî | Enable the real-time ClickHouse dual-sink: the indexer writes each ledger's structural extract to CH inline (non-blocking), keeping the lake within ~seconds of the chain. ON by default (ADR-0041): the certified-lake substrate backs the coverage claim, the CH completeness path, and lake-derived supply ‚Äî opt out only on deployments that cannot run ClickHouse, accepting the loss of all three. |
| `storage.clickhouse_projector_source` | `bool` | `true` | ‚Äî | Feed-switch: the projector reads forward events from the ClickHouse lake (contract_events) instead of Postgres soroban_events, enabling soroban_events decommission. Requires clickhouse_live_sink. ON by default (ADR-0041), matching the production topology. |

### `[ingestion]`

| Key | Type | Default | Env override | Description |
| --- | ---- | ------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| `ingestion.enabled_sources` | `[]string` | `["soroswap","aquarius","phoenix"]` | ‚Äî | List of source connector names to run on this indexer replica. See config.KnownSources for valid values. |
| `ingestion.backfill_from_ledger` | `uint32` | `0` | ‚Äî | Earliest ledger to backfill from; 0 = continue-from-persisted-cursor. |
| `ingestion.backfill_batch_size` | `uint32` | `64` | ‚Äî | Ledgers per backfill fetch batch. |
| `ingestion.cursor_store_scheme` | `string` | `postgres` | ‚Äî | Where per-source cursors live ‚Äî postgres / redis. |
| `ingestion.live_seam_ledger` | `uint32` | `0` | ‚Äî | First ledger in the live bucket. Below this, indexer reads from galexie-archive. 0 disables the archive bucket entirely. |
| `ingestion.projector.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Master switch. When false the projector goroutines are not started. |
| `ingestion.projector.persist_per_source` | `bool` | `true` | ‚Äî | When false (Phase 4+), the dispatcher's events-goroutine skips Soroban-derived events so the projector is sole writer. Requires Enabled=true. Defaults true (Phase 3 parallel mode); operator flips to false once projector lag is verified low. The sep41 domain is exempt ‚Äî the projector is always its sole writer (F-1316 / TASK #16b). |

| `oracle.band.standard_reference_contract` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Band Protocol StandardReference contract (C-prefix) on mainnet ‚Äî CCQXWMZVM3KRTXTUPTN53YHL272QGKF32L7XEDNZ2S6OSUFK3NFBGG5M. |
| `oracle.soroswap.factory_contract` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Soroswap factory contract (C-prefix) on mainnet ‚Äî CA4HEQTL2WPEUYKYKCDOHCDNIV4QHNJ7EL4J4NQ6VADP7SYHVRYZ7AW2. |
| `oracle.soroswap.seed_rpc_endpoint` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | stellar-rpc URL used for the boot-time factory sweep. Any public pubnet endpoint works (e.g. https://mainnet.sorobanrpc.com). Falls back to stellar.rpc_endpoints[0] when empty. |

### `[external]`

| Key | Type | Default | Env override | Description |
| --- | ---- | ------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| `external.binance.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Whether this connector runs. Off by default ‚Äî no network egress until operator opts in. |
| `external.binance.poll_interval` | `int64` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Override the connector's built-in default poll cadence (e.g. "120s"). Empty/zero uses the connector default. |
| `external.kraken.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Whether this connector runs. Off by default ‚Äî no network egress until operator opts in. |
| `external.kraken.poll_interval` | `int64` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Override the connector's built-in default poll cadence (e.g. "120s"). Empty/zero uses the connector default. |
| `external.bitstamp.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Whether this connector runs. Off by default ‚Äî no network egress until operator opts in. |
| `external.bitstamp.poll_interval` | `int64` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Override the connector's built-in default poll cadence (e.g. "120s"). Empty/zero uses the connector default. |
| `external.coinbase.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Whether this connector runs. Off by default ‚Äî no network egress until operator opts in. |
| `external.coinbase.poll_interval` | `int64` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Override the connector's built-in default poll cadence (e.g. "120s"). Empty/zero uses the connector default. |
| `external.exchangeratesapi.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Whether this connector runs. Off by default. |
| `external.exchangeratesapi.api_key` | `string` | _(required)_ | `EXCHANGERATESAPI_KEY` | ExchangeRatesApi access key. Prefer env var; TOML fallback exists for local-dev convenience. |
| `external.exchangeratesapi.base` | `string` | `USD` | ‚Äî | Base currency (USD, EUR, GBP, ‚Ä¶). Defaults to USD. Free tier locked to EUR; paid tier accepts any allow-listed fiat. |
| `external.polygon_forex.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Whether this connector runs. Off by default. |
| `external.polygon_forex.api_key` | `string` | _(required)_ | `POLYGON_API_KEY` | Polygon.io API key. Prefer env var POLYGON_API_KEY; TOML fallback for local-dev only. |
| `external.polygon_forex.base` | `string` | `USD` | ‚Äî | Base currency filter. Only tickers matching C:<base><quote> emit. Defaults to USD. |
| `external.coingecko.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Whether this connector runs. Off by default ‚Äî no network egress until operator opts in. |
| `external.coingecko.poll_interval` | `int64` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Override the connector's built-in default poll cadence (e.g. "120s"). Empty/zero uses the connector default. |
| `external.coinmarketcap.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Whether this connector runs. Off by default. |
| `external.coinmarketcap.api_key` | `string` | _(required)_ | `COINMARKETCAP_API_KEY` | CMC Pro API key, passed as X-CMC_PRO_API_KEY header. Prefer env var. |
| `external.cryptocompare.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Whether this connector runs. Off by default. |
| `external.cryptocompare.api_key` | `string` | _(required)_ | `CRYPTOCOMPARE_API_KEY` | CryptoCompare API key, passed as 'Authorization: Apikey <KEY>'. Prefer env var. |
| `external.ecb.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Whether this connector runs. Off by default ‚Äî no network egress until operator opts in. |
| `external.ecb.poll_interval` | `int64` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Override the connector's built-in default poll cadence (e.g. "120s"). Empty/zero uses the connector default. |
| `external.chainlink.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Whether the Chainlink ingest poller runs. Off by default. |
| `external.chainlink.rpc_url` | `string` | _(required)_ | `CHAINLINK_RPC_URL` | Ethereum mainnet JSON-RPC endpoint (Alchemy / Infura / public). For Alchemy this includes the API key in the URL path (.../v2/<KEY>) ‚Äî treat the whole value as a secret. Prefer env var. |
| `external.chainlink.poll_interval` | `int64` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Override the default 30s poll cadence. Empty/zero uses the package default. |
| `external.chainlink.feed_map` | `map[string]ChainlinkFeedSetting` | `{}` | ‚Äî | Maps canonical pair string ('crypto:BTC/fiat:USD' etc.) to the AggregatorV3 contract address + decimals + invert. Empty falls back to the built-in default covering BTC/ETH/LINK/EUR/GBP/JPY vs USD. |
| `external.chainlink.feed_map.<key>.address` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | 0x-prefixed AggregatorV3 contract address on Ethereum mainnet. |
| `external.chainlink.feed_map.<key>.decimals` | `uint8` | `8` | ‚Äî | Power-of-10 divisor for the raw int256 answer. Defaults to 8 (Chainlink's standard). Operator sets per-feed only when the feed publishes at a non-standard scale. |
| `external.chainlink.feed_map.<key>.invert` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | If true, the canonical pair is the reciprocal of the feed's natural quote ‚Äî e.g. operator wants USD/EUR but the feed publishes EUR/USD. price ‚Üí 1/price after scaling. |

### `[aggregate]`

| Key | Type | Default | Env override | Description |
| --- | ---- | ------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| `aggregate.vwap_window_seconds` | `int` | `300` | ‚Äî | Rolling VWAP window in seconds. |
| `aggregate.twap_window_seconds` | `int` | `300` | ‚Äî | Rolling TWAP window in seconds (fallback when volume below threshold). |
| `aggregate.min_usd_volume` | `float64` | `10000` | ‚Äî | Per-pair minimum USD volume within the window for VWAP eligibility. |
| `aggregate.outlier_sigma_threshold` | `float64` | `4` | ‚Äî | Reject trades priced more than N standard deviations from the per-window (unweighted) MEAN before VWAP. Implementation is mean+stdev computed per window (internal/aggregate/outliers.go), NOT a rolling median or MAD; 0 disables it and fewer than 3 valid prices is a no-op. |
| `aggregate.triangulation_enabled` | `bool` | `true` | ‚Äî | Master switch for the post-refresh triangulation pass. When true (default), the aggregator runs each aggregate.triangulations chain √ó window after the per-pair refresh, multiplying the leg VWAPs and writing the implied target price. When false, the pass is skipped entirely regardless of aggregate.triangulations entries ‚Äî an operator-side kill-switch for the triangulation feature without having to clear the chain table. |
| `aggregate.interval_seconds` | `int` | `30` | ‚Äî | Tick cadence ‚Äî gap between successive (pair, window) refresh passes. 0 falls back to the library default (30s). |
| `aggregate.divergence_min_interval_seconds` | `int` | `300` | ‚Äî | Minimum wall-clock seconds between divergence-refresh passes. Tick still fires at interval_seconds, but the divergence pass is skipped if elapsed < this value. Default 300s (= cachekeys.DivergenceTTL) keeps the API's div:<asset> cache continuously populated while burning ~10√ó less of the CMC monthly-quota (F-0030 follow-up). Set to 0 to refresh every tick (legacy). |
| `aggregate.max_trades_per_window` | `int` | `10000` | ‚Äî | Per-(pair, window) cap on TradesInRange row count to bound a runaway scan. 0 falls back to the library default (10000). |
| `aggregate.disable_class_filter` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Disable the default ClassExchange-only VWAP filter so every fetched trade contributes regardless of source class. Off by default ‚Äî see internal/sources/external/registry.go for class semantics. |
| `aggregate.enable_stablecoin_fiat_proxy` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Expand fiat-denominated target pairs to include stablecoin backers (XLM/fiat:USD also pulls XLM/USDT/USDC/DAI/PYUSD/USDP and collapses onto the target). Off by default ‚Äî N+1 TradesInRange calls per (pair, window). |
| `aggregate.pairs` | `[]string` | `[]` | ‚Äî | Aggregator coverage set as canonical pair strings ("crypto:XLM/fiat:USD", "native/USDC-G‚Ä¶"). Empty leaves the binary's built-in default (XLM/BTC/ETH √ó USD/EUR/GBP). Each entry is parsed via canonical.ParseAsset on both sides; an unparseable entry fails Validate. |
| `aggregate.windows` | `[]string` | `[]` | ‚Äî | Per-window cadences as Go time.Duration strings ("5m", "1h", "24h"). Empty leaves the orchestrator's built-in default ([5m, 1h, 24h]). |
| `aggregate.triangulations` | `[]struct` | `[]` | ‚Äî | Operator-configured chain pricing entries ‚Äî each row defines a target pair plus an ordered chain of leg pairs. After the per-pair refresh runs, the orchestrator multiplies each leg's freshly-cached VWAP via aggregate.TriangulateChain and writes the implied target VWAP to its own cache key. Empty (default) skips triangulation entirely. |
| `aggregate.triangulations[].target` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Implied target pair (canonical wire form). |
| `aggregate.triangulations[].legs` | `[]string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Ordered chain of leg pairs; product yields the target price. Must have at least 2 entries and adjacent legs must share their pivot asset. |

### `[anomaly]`

| Key | Type | Default | Env override | Description |
| --- | ---- | ------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| `anomaly.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Master switch. When false, anomaly checks are disabled and every bucket is published as-is. Flip to true after operator has classified the asset set. |
| `anomaly.thresholds` | `map[string]AnomalyThreshold` | `{}` | ‚Äî | Per-class threshold table. Keys are asset class names (stablecoin/treasury/crypto/governance/default). Empty falls back to package defaults; partial maps merge over defaults. The default row is required (loader fills it from package defaults if absent). |
| `anomaly.thresholds.<key>.warn_pct` | `float64` | `30.0` | ‚Äî | Deviation above this percentage triggers ActionWarn (publish with divergence_warning flag). |
| `anomaly.thresholds.<key>.freeze_pct` | `float64` | `75.0` | ‚Äî | Deviation above this percentage triggers ActionFreeze when source_count<=1 (don't publish; serve last-known-good). |
| `anomaly.classifications` | `map` | `{}` | ‚Äî | Operator-curated map of canonical asset_id ‚Üí asset class (stablecoin/treasury/crypto/governance). Anything absent falls through to the default class. |
| `anomaly.phase2.confidence_max_freeze` | `float64` | `0.10` | ‚Äî | Freeze fires when confidence is strictly less than this. ADR-0019 default 0.10. |
| `anomaly.phase2.z_score_min_freeze` | `float64` | `5.0` | ‚Äî | Freeze fires when z-score is strictly greater than this. ADR-0019 default 5.0 (the documented 5œÉ trigger). |
| `anomaly.phase2.source_count_max_freeze` | `int` | `1` | ‚Äî | Freeze fires when source count is at or below this. ADR-0019 default 1 (single-source pattern). |

### `[api]`

| Key | Type | Default | Env override | Description |
| --- | ---- | ------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| `api.listen_addr` | `string` | `0.0.0.0:3000` | ‚Äî | Bind address for the HTTP server. |
| `api.external_base_url` | `string` | `https://api.stellarindex.io/v1` | ‚Äî | Public-facing base URL (e.g. https://api.stellarindex.io/v1). |
| `api.tls_cert_probe_hosts` | `[]string` | `["api.stellarindex.io","status.stellarindex.io","stellarindex.io"]` | ‚Äî | Public hostnames whose TLS leaf cert NotAfter the API binary should periodically probe and surface as stellarindex_tls_cert_not_after_unix{host}. Each entry may include :port; bare hostnames default to :443. The probe goroutine ticks every 6h. F-0051 (audit-2026-05-26): Caddy auto-renews Let's Encrypt 30d before expiry but silent renewal failures (DNS, rate limit, ACME quota) would otherwise only surface at cert expiry. Empty list disables the probe. |
| `api.auth_mode` | `string` | `none` | ‚Äî | Authentication mode ‚Äî none / apikey / apikey_optional / sep10. 'none' attaches anonymous Subject to every request. 'apikey' requires Authorization: Bearer <key> on every request; missing ‚Üí 401. 'apikey_optional' is the freemium shape ‚Äî anonymous floor (60/min) without a key, per-key tier (1000/min default) with a valid key, invalid key ‚Üí 401. 'sep10' requires a SEP-10 JWT. The API binary wires real validators when the required dependencies are present; deployments that opt into auth without satisfying those fail loud rather than silently demoting to anonymous. |
| `api.auth_backend` | `string` | `redis` | ‚Äî | Backing store for API-key validation. 'redis' (default) uses the legacy apikey:<hash> JSON records minted by /v1/signup. 'postgres' uses the platform.api_keys table (the dashboard's source of truth) with Redis as a read-through cache ‚Äî required for keys minted from the dashboard to authenticate against the runtime API. Cutover knob: deployments running both /v1/signup keys and dashboard-minted keys should use 'postgres' (the validator falls back to Postgres on Redis cache miss + writes back, so existing legacy keys keep working transparently). CUTOVER PROCEDURE ‚Äî this is the hot auth path on a live API, so flip after a soak, not blind: (1) leave 'redis' running and confirm the dashboard bundle is wired (api.dashboard.base_url set, Postgres reachable) ‚Äî the Postgres validator is constructed regardless of this flag, so its InvalidateCachedKey path is already active on dashboard revoke; (2) flip a canary instance to 'postgres' and watch that authenticated traffic still 200s and that dashboard-minted keys now authenticate; (3) soak, then roll the fleet. ROLLBACK is instant and lossless: set 'redis' and restart ‚Äî no data migration either direction (Postgres stays the dashboard's source of truth, Redis keeps the legacy /v1/signup records; the two populations coexist). Invalidation on revoke/update works in BOTH modes: 'redis' rewrites the canonical record in place; 'postgres' evicts the read-through cache entry (dashboard revoke + Stripe tier-upgrade both call InvalidateCachedKey). NOTE: 'postgres' disables the legacy /v1/account/keys self-service surface (it writes only to Redis, which the Postgres validator does not read as canonical) ‚Äî customers manage keys via /v1/dashboard/keys instead. |
| `api.anon_rate_limit_per_min` | `int` | `60` | ‚Äî | Per-IP rate limit for anonymous requests. |
| `api.key_rate_limit_per_min` | `int` | `1000` | ‚Äî | Per-API-key rate limit, default tier. |
| `api.signup_require_email_verification` | `bool` | `true` | ‚Äî | F-1218: when true, /v1/signup-minted API keys must complete email-ownership-proof (clicking the link emailed at signup) before they can authenticate. Default true (2026-05-13): we are still pre-launch with no consumer traffic, so the safe default is to require verification ‚Äî operators who want to allow unverified signup must opt in explicitly. Pre-launch default-flip narrows the launch-blocker surface; F-1218 closure required this. |
| `api.cdn_enabled` | `bool` | `true` | ‚Äî | Emit CDN-friendly Cache-Control headers on long-immutable endpoints. |
| `api.allowed_origins` | `[]string` | `["*"]` | ‚Äî | CORS allow-list for browser clients. |
| `api.allow_credentials` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Emit Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true on CORS responses to allowed origins. Required for cookie-bearing cross-origin requests (magic-link session on /v1/account/me, /v1/account/keys). Browser-incompatible with allowed_origins=["*"]; the API panics at boot if both are set. |
| `api.trusted_proxy_cidrs` | `[]string` | `[]` | ‚Äî | Immediate peer CIDR allow-list that is permitted to supply X-Forwarded-For. Empty means the API ignores that header and uses the socket peer address for logging, anonymous identity, and IP-based rate limiting. |
| `api.sep10.seed_env` | `string` | `STELLARINDEX_SEP10_SEED` | ‚Äî | Environment variable holding the server signing keypair S-strkey. Operators rotate this on a schedule; ansible-vault stores the actual value. |
| `api.sep10.jwt_secret_env` | `string` | `STELLARINDEX_SEP10_JWT_SECRET` | ‚Äî | Environment variable holding the HMAC-SHA256 JWT secret (‚â• 32 bytes of entropy required). |
| `api.sep10.web_auth_domain` | `string` | `api.stellarindex.io` | ‚Äî | SEP-10 web_auth_domain ‚Äî the host that serves /v1/auth/sep10/*. Carried inside the challenge tx so clients verify before signing. Typically the API's external host (e.g. api.stellarindex.io). |
| `api.sep10.home_domain` | `string` | `stellarindex.io` | ‚Äî | Issuer home_domain. Carried in the JWT iss claim and in the challenge's first manage_data op. Typically same as the project root domain. |
| `api.sep10.challenge_ttl` | `int64` | `15m` | ‚Äî | How long a SEP-10 challenge is valid for signing. SDK requires ‚â• 1s; SEP-10 spec recommends 15m. |
| `api.sep10.jwt_ttl` | `int64` | `1h` | ‚Äî | Lifetime of an issued JWT. Clients refresh by repeating the challenge ‚Üí verify flow. |
| `api.streaming.pairs` | `[][]string` | `[]` | ‚Äî | Operator-declared closed-bucket fanout pair list. Each entry is a two-element [base, quote] array of canonical asset strings (e.g. [["native", "fiat:USD"], ["credit:USDC:GA5Z‚Ä¶", "fiat:USD"]]). Empty disables the producer; clients that connect see SSE open + heartbeats but no price_update events. |
| `api.streaming.poll_interval` | `int64` | `5s` | ‚Äî | Per-pair poll cadence for the closed-bucket producer. Default 5s; clamped to 1s minimum. |
| `api.stripe.signing_secret` | `string` | _(required)_ | `STELLARINDEX_STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` | Stripe webhook signing secret (whsec_‚Ä¶). Empty disables the endpoint. |
| `api.prometheus_url` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Prometheus HTTP API root (e.g. http://localhost:9090) backing /v1/status. Empty leaves /v1/status serving an in-process surface (uptime + region only). |
| `api.archive_report_path` | `string` | `/var/lib/galexie/last-completeness-report.json` | ‚Äî | Filesystem path of the archive-completeness daemon's latest JSON report (the -output-file of 'stellarindex-ops archive-completeness verify'; the systemd unit writes /var/lib/galexie/last-completeness-report.json). Backs GET /v1/diagnostics/archive. The endpoint 404s while the file doesn't exist yet and 503s when this is empty. |
| `api.dashboard.base_url` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Absolute URL of the explorer hosting the in-site dashboard (e.g. https://stellarindex.io). The magic-link callback URL embedded in emails is {base_url}/auth/callback?token=<plaintext>, and the post-login redirect lands on {base_url}/account. |
| `api.dashboard.email_from` | `string` | `Stellar Index <hello@stellarindex.io>` | ‚Äî | From: address for transactional emails (e.g. 'Stellar Index <hello@stellarindex.io>'). Must match a domain Resend has verified for the configured API key. |
| `api.dashboard.resend_api_key_env` | `string` | `STELLARINDEX_RESEND_API_KEY` | ‚Äî | Environment variable holding the Resend transactional-email API key (re_‚Ä¶). Empty value leaves the dashboard auth flow on a NoopSender ‚Äî magic-link tokens land in the API logs only, useful for local dev. Production sets this. |
| `api.dashboard.magic_link_ttl_minutes` | `int` | `15` | ‚Äî | Magic-link validity in minutes. Default 15 ‚Äî long enough for an email to arrive + the user to switch contexts; short enough to limit replay-window if a phone is briefly unattended. |
| `api.dashboard.session_ttl_days` | `int` | `30` | ‚Äî | Session-cookie lifetime in days. Default 30 ‚Äî matches typical SaaS dashboards; users sign in monthly without re-authing. |
| `api.dashboard.cookie_secure` | `bool` | `true` | ‚Äî | Set the Secure flag on the session cookie. Production = true; dev (http://localhost) = false. |
| `api.dashboard.cookie_domain` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Cookie Domain attribute. Empty (default) means a host-only cookie scoped to the API host. Set to '.stellarindex.io' if a future surface needs the cookie shared across subdomains. |

### `[metadata]`

| Key | Type | Default | Env override | Description |
| --- | ---- | ------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| `metadata.issuer_home_domains` | `map` | `{}` | ‚Äî | Static curated map of issuer-account G-strkey ‚Üí home-domain. Layered behind the live LCM-derived resolver as a fallback for issuers whose AccountEntry hasn't been observed yet (or whose on-chain home_domain field is empty). |

### `[supply]`

| Key | Type | Default | Env override | Description |
| --- | ---- | ------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| `supply.sdf_reserve_accounts` | `[]string` | `[]` | ‚Äî | G-strkey list of SDF-controlled reserve accounts whose XLM balances are excluded from circulating supply per ADR-0011 Algorithm 1. |
| `supply.reserve_balances_stroops` | `map` | `{}` | ‚Äî | Operator-managed snapshot of each SDF reserve account's XLM balance in stroops (decimal string). Updated manually on SDF reserve-move announcements. Used as the fallback source when the LCM AccountEntry observer (Task #54) hasn't yet populated account_observations for the watched reserve set; the live observer takes over once those rows land. |
| `supply.aggregator_refresh_enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Run the supply-snapshot writer as a goroutine in the aggregator instead of via the systemd timer. Requires the LCM AccountEntry observer to be backfilled across the watched accounts (or the static reserve_balances_stroops fallback to be valid). |
| `supply.aggregator_refresh_cadence` | `int64` | `5m` | ‚Äî | Per-cycle interval for the in-aggregator supply-snapshot worker (only used when aggregator_refresh_enabled is true). |
| `supply.watched_classic_assets` | `[]string` | `[]` | ‚Äî | Operator-curated classic credit assets (CODE-ISSUER form) to track for Algorithm 2 supply per ADR-0022. Empty leaves the classic-supply pipeline off. |
| `supply.sac_wrappers` | `map` | `{}` | ‚Äî | SAC wrapper contract C-strkey ‚Üí supply.AssetKey (CODE:ISSUER) map. Drives the SAC balance observer's watched-contract filter. Pure SEP-41 contracts reuse this map by mapping contract_id ‚Üí contract_id. |
| `supply.watched_sep41_contracts` | `[]string` | `[]` | ‚Äî | Operator-curated SEP-41 Soroban contract C-strkeys to track for Algorithm 3 supply per ADR-0023. Empty leaves the SEP-41 supply pipeline off. |
| `supply.strict_freshness_required` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | F-1236: when true, supply snapshots without a MinComponentLedger anchor (i.e. zero-value freshness, the static-XLM fallback or a transiently-failing producer) are rejected rather than published. Default false preserves backwards-compatible permissive behaviour; flip true after the freshness producers are confirmed wired in steady state. |
| `supply.stale_component_ledgers_by_asset` | `map` | `{}` | ‚Äî | Per-asset override of the F-1236 stale-component-ledger threshold. Map keys are asset_key (CODE-ISSUER for classic, bare contract id for SEP-41); values are ledger counts. Empty map (default) keeps every asset on the global 1000-ledger threshold. F-0040 (audit-2026-05-26). |

### `[trades]`

| Key | Type | Default | Env override | Description |
| --- | ---- | ------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| `trades.usd_pegged_classic_assets` | `[]string` | `[]` | ‚Äî | Classic credit asset_keys (CODE-ISSUER) the operator declares as USD-pegged stablecoins. On-chain DEX trades quoted in these (or their SAC wrappers, transitive via [supply.sac_wrappers]) populate trades.usd_volume at insert time. Empty preserves the off-chain-only default. |

### `[divergence]`

| Key | Type | Default | Env override | Description |
| --- | ---- | ------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| `divergence.threshold_pct` | `float64` | `5.0` | ‚Äî | Divergence percentage above which the warning flag fires. |
| `divergence.min_sources_for_warning` | `int` | `2` | ‚Äî | Minimum successful references before warning_fired can be true. |
| `divergence.per_reference_timeout_seconds` | `int` | `5` | ‚Äî | Bound for each reference call. Default 5. |
| `divergence.coingecko.enabled` | `bool` | `true` | ‚Äî | Whether the CoinGecko reference is wired into the divergence service. |
| `divergence.coingecko.base_url` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | CoinGecko API base URL. Empty defaults to https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3. |
| `divergence.coingecko.id_map` | `map` | `{}` | ‚Äî | Maps canonical asset_id ‚Üí CoinGecko slug. Operator-curated; empty falls back to the built-in default covering XLM + major stables. |
| `divergence.chainlink.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Whether the Chainlink reference is wired into the divergence service. |
| `divergence.chainlink.rpc_url` | `string` | _(required)_ | `CHAINLINK_RPC_URL` | Ethereum JSON-RPC endpoint. Shares the CHAINLINK_RPC_URL env var with the ingest poller (env overrides TOML). Empty defaults to https://cloudflare-eth.com. |
| `divergence.chainlink.feeds` | `map[string]ChainlinkFeedConfig` | `{}` | ‚Äî | Maps pair strings to {address, decimals, invert}. Empty disables Chainlink in practice. |
| `divergence.chainlink.feeds.<key>.address` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | 0x-prefixed mainnet feed contract address. |
| `divergence.chainlink.feeds.<key>.decimals` | `int` | `8` | ‚Äî | Power-of-10 divisor for the raw int256. Defaults to 8 (Chainlink standard). |
| `divergence.chainlink.feeds.<key>.invert` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Set true when canonical pair is reciprocal of the feed's natural quote. |
| `divergence.chainlink.feeds.<key>.max_age_hours` | `int` | `0` | ‚Äî | Staleness ceiling in hours for the feed's latestRoundData updatedAt; rounds older than this are rejected as reference-unavailable (CS-089). 0 = 3h crypto default; use ~76 for FX feeds (they pause over market closes). |
| `divergence.reflector.enabled` | `bool` | `true` | ‚Äî | Whether this on-chain oracle reference is wired into the divergence service. |
| `divergence.reflector.max_age_minutes` | `int` | `0` | ‚Äî | Staleness ceiling in minutes for the oracle's latest observation; older observations are rejected as reference-unavailable. 0 = per-oracle default (Reflector 30m, Redstone/Band 26h). |
| `divergence.redstone.enabled` | `bool` | `true` | ‚Äî | Whether this on-chain oracle reference is wired into the divergence service. |
| `divergence.redstone.max_age_minutes` | `int` | `0` | ‚Äî | Staleness ceiling in minutes for the oracle's latest observation; older observations are rejected as reference-unavailable. 0 = per-oracle default (Reflector 30m, Redstone/Band 26h). |
| `divergence.band.enabled` | `bool` | `true` | ‚Äî | Whether this on-chain oracle reference is wired into the divergence service. |
| `divergence.band.max_age_minutes` | `int` | `0` | ‚Äî | Staleness ceiling in minutes for the oracle's latest observation; older observations are rejected as reference-unavailable. 0 = per-oracle default (Reflector 30m, Redstone/Band 26h). |
| `divergence.supply.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Whether the supply cross-check worker runs. Off by default (makes outbound HTTP calls; opt in on r1 via ansible). |
| `divergence.supply.refresh_interval_seconds` | `int` | `900` | ‚Äî | Per-cycle interval for the supply cross-check worker. Supply moves slowly, so a slow cadence is fine. Default 900 (15 min). |
| `divergence.supply.threshold_pct` | `float64` | `1.0` | ‚Äî | Relative-divergence percentage above which a supply cross-check reads 'divergent' and the alert fires. Default 1.0 (well above the ~0.03% XLM noise floor). |
| `divergence.supply.per_reference_timeout_seconds` | `int` | `10` | ‚Äî | Bound for each supply-reference HTTP call. Default 10. |
| `divergence.supply.dashboard.enabled` | `bool` | `true` | ‚Äî | Whether the Stellar Dashboard supply reference is consulted. On by default within [divergence.supply]. |
| `divergence.supply.dashboard.base_url` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | Dashboard API base. Empty defaults to https://dashboard.stellar.org/api/v3. The reference GETs base_url + /lumens. |
| `divergence.supply.coingecko.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Whether the CoinGecko supply reference is consulted. Off by default (free tier 429-throttled). |
| `divergence.supply.coingecko.api_key` | `string` | _(required)_ | `COINGECKO_API_KEY` | CoinGecko Pro API key, sent as x-cg-pro-api-key. Prefer env var COINGECKO_API_KEY. |
| `divergence.supply.coingecko.base_url` | `string` | _(required)_ | ‚Äî | CoinGecko API base. Empty defaults to https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3. |
| `divergence.supply.coingecko.id_map` | `map` | `{}` | ‚Äî | Maps canonical asset_id ‚Üí CoinGecko coin id for the supply lookup. Empty falls back to the built-in default (native/crypto:XLM ‚Üí stellar). |

### `[price_alerts]`

| Key | Type | Default | Env override | Description |
| --- | ---- | ------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| `price_alerts.enabled` | `bool` | `false` | ‚Äî | Start the price-alert evaluator loop in the aggregator. Off by default. |
| `price_alerts.interval_seconds` | `int` | `30` | ‚Äî | Sweep cadence in seconds between price-alert evaluation passes. 0 = library default (30s). |

### `[signup_reaper]`

| Key | Type | Default | Env override | Description |
| --- | ---- | ------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| `signup_reaper.enabled` | `bool` | `true` | ‚Äî | Start the speculative-account reaper loop in the API binary. On by default ‚Äî the reaped rows (Suspended signup-race orphans with no user/key) are pure garbage. Set false to disable. |
| `signup_reaper.interval_minutes` | `int` | `60` | ‚Äî | Minutes between reaper sweeps. 0 = library default (60). |
| `signup_reaper.min_age_minutes` | `int` | `1440` | ‚Äî | Minimum minutes a suspended orphan must age before the reaper deletes it (safety window). 0 = library default (1440 = 24h). |

### `[obs]`

| Key | Type | Default | Env override | Description |
| --- | ---- | ------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| `obs.metrics_listen` | `string` | `127.0.0.1:9464` | ‚Äî | Bind address for the dedicated /metrics Prometheus endpoint. Read by the indexer, the aggregator, and the long-lived ops binaries (cross-region-monitor, verify-archive --metrics). The API binary serves /metrics on its public listener and ignores this field. |
| `obs.log_level` | `string` | `info` | ‚Äî | Minimum log level ‚Äî debug / info / warn / error. |
| `obs.log_format` | `string` | `json` | ‚Äî | Log format ‚Äî json / console. |
| `obs.trace_exporter` | `string` | `none` | ‚Äî | OpenTelemetry trace exporter. Currently only 'none' is wired in this build; the 'otlp' value is reserved for the future tracing rollout and is rejected by Validate() until the exporter is implemented (so an operator setting it doesn't think tracing is on when it isn't). |
| `obs.trace_sample` | `float64` | `0.1` | ‚Äî | Trace sampling ratio ‚Äî 0.0 (none) to 1.0 (all). Read by the future tracing rollout; ignored in this build. |
